what: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: Pure-C runtime SIMD dispatch templates R packages. Compile scalar, SSE, AVX, AVX-512, NEON C translation units separately; switch among compiled CPU-supported implementations runtime without unsafe overrides.

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: GitHub:

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: Add RsimdDispatch header/template provider: copy C dispatch scaffold package root: Replace demo count_nonzero() kernels scalar/SSE/AVX/NEON kernels. Keep R API, CPU detection, dispatcher baseline compiler flags; ISA-specific translation units get flags -mavx2 -mavx512f -mavx512bw -mavx512vl. See package vignettes downstream-package workflow dispatch semantics.

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: simd_set_backend() rejects uncompiled CPU-unsupported backends. unsafe override.
code: library(RsimdDispatch)  x <- as.raw(c(0, 1, 2, 0, 255))  count_nonzero(x) #> [1] 3 simd_backend() #> [1] "avx2" simd_set_backend("scalar") simd_backend() #> [1] "scalar" simd_set_backend("avx2") simd_backend() #> [1] "avx2" simd_set_backend("auto") simd_info()[c("compiled_backends", "cpu_supported_backends", "available_backends")] #> $compiled_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2"   "avx512" #>  #> $cpu_supported_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2"   #>  #> $available_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2" simd_dispatch_template_path() #> [1] "/usr/local/lib/R/site-library/RsimdDispatch/templates/dispatch-c"

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: Switching backends -process variants linked one R shared library selected guarded function pointers.
code: count_nonzero(x) #> [1] 3 simd_info()[c("selected_backend", "available_backends")] #> $selected_backend #> [1] "avx2" #>  #> $available_backends #> [1] "scalar" "sse2"   "sse41"  "avx2" simd_set_backend("scalar") scalar_count <- count_nonzero(x)  simd_set_backend("avx2") avx2_count <- count_nonzero(x)  simd_set_backend("auto") data.frame(   scalar = scalar_count,   avx2 = avx2_count,   selected_after_auto = simd_backend() ) #>   scalar avx2 selected_after_auto #> 1      3    3                avx2

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
code: set.seed(1) x <- as.raw(sample.int(256L, 50 * 1024 * 1024, replace = TRUE) - 1L)  mark <- bench::mark(   scalar = {     simd_set_backend("scalar")     count_nonzero(x)   },   avx2 = {     simd_set_backend("avx2")     count_nonzero(x)   },   iterations = 20,   check = TRUE,   memory = FALSE )  simd_set_backend("auto")  bench <- data.frame(   backend = as.character(mark$expression),   median_ms = as.numeric(mark$median) * 1000,   mb_per_second = length(x) * as.numeric(mark$`itr/sec`) / 1e6,   iterations = mark$n_itr,   row.names = NULL ) bench$speedup_vs_scalar <- bench$mb_per_second / bench$mb_per_second[bench$backend == "scalar"]  knitr::kable(bench, digits = 3)

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: maintainer workflow RsimdDispatch , something downstream packages need run. Downstream packages use LinkingTo: RsimdDispatch copied dispatch template; re-vendor SIMDe. RsimdDispatch vendors full SIMDe header tree inst/include/simde. update pinned checkout regenerate bundled-code notices: Provenance lives inst/vendor/simde/VERSION, inst/AUTHORS, inst/LICENCE.note.

title: Runtime SIMD Dispatch Templates for C Code in R Packages
text: top-level Makefile uses GNU make.
code: make rd       # roxygen docs and NAMESPACE make readme   # evaluate README.Rmd and write README.md make test make check

what: Count non-zero bytes with the selected SIMD backend — count_nonzero
title: Count non-zero bytes with the selected SIMD backend — count_nonzero
text: Demonstration kernel runtime dispatch template. count_nonzero() counts bytes 00 raw vector using currently selected backend. default backend "auto", selects best compiled backend supported current CPU/runtime.

what: Locate or copy the C runtime dispatch template — simd_dispatch_template_path
title: Locate or copy the C runtime dispatch template — simd_dispatch_template_path
text: simd_dispatch_template_path() returns installed template directory. use_simd_dispatch() copies template package directory. deliberately small helper; downstream packages review rename generated symbols kernels.

what: Report runtime SIMD dispatch diagnostics — simd_info
title: Report runtime SIMD dispatch diagnostics — simd_info
text: Returns requested backend, selected backend, compiled backends, CPU-supported backends, target information. Calling initializes lazy auto-dispatch selection already initialized.

title: Report runtime SIMD dispatch diagnostics — simd_info
Report runtime SIMD dispatch diagnostics — simd_info
text: named list dispatch CPU feature diagnostics. Backend-set entries character vectors, comma-separated strings.

title: Report runtime SIMD dispatch diagnostics — simd_info

what: Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend
title: Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend
text: Select backend used subsequent calls count_nonzero(). RsimdDispatch keeps compiled variants one shared object switches guarded function pointers. makes -process benchmarking possible.

title: Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend
Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend
text: backend One "auto", "scalar", "sse2", "sse41", "avx2", "avx512", "neon".

title: Select the runtime SIMD backend — simd_set_backend
text: selected backend, invisibly. "auto", backend chosen compiled CPU-supported set.
